chunks.embed:
***TABLE R602.3(1)**  
**FASTENER SCHEDULE FOR STRUCTURAL MEMBERS***

| DESCRIPTION OF BUILDING ELEMENTS | NUMBER AND TYPE OF FASTENERa,b,c | SPACING OF FASTENERS |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Joist to sill or girder, toe nail | 3-8d (2-1/2" × 0.113") | — |
| 1" × 6" subfloor or less to each joist, face nail | 2-8d (21/2" × 0.113")   2 staples, 13/4" | —   — |
| 2" subfloor to joist or girder, blind and face nail | 2-16d (31/2" × 0.135") | — |
| Sole plate to joist or blocking, face nail | 16d (31/2" × 0.135") | 16" o.c. |
| Top or sole plate to stud, end nail | 2-16d (31/2" × 0.135") | — |
| Stud to sole plate, toe nail | 3-8d (21/2" × 0.113") or   2-16d (31/2" × 0.135") | — |
| Double studs, face nail | 10d (3" × 0.128") | 24" o.c. |
| Double top plates, face nail | 10d (3" × 0.128") | 24" o.c. |
| Sole plate to joist or blocking at braced wall panels | 3-16d (31/2" × 0.135") | 16" o.c. |
| Double top plates, minimum 24-inch offset of end joints, face nail in lapped area | 8-16d (31/2" × 0.135") | — |
| Blocking between joists or rafters to top plate, toe nail | 3-8d (21/2" × 0.113") | — |
| Rim joist to top plate, toe nail | 8d (21/2" × 0.113") | 6" o.c. |
| Top plates, laps at corners and intersections, face nail | 2-10d (3" × 0.128") | — |
| Built-up header, two pieces with 1/2" spacer | 16d (31/2" × 0.135") | 16" o.c. along each edge |
| Continued header, two pieces | 16d (31/2" × 0.135") | 16" o.c. along each edge |
| Ceiling joists to plate, toe nail | 3-8d (21/2" × 0.113") | — |
| Continuous header to stud, toe nail | 4-8d (21/2" × 0.113") | — |
| Ceiling joist, laps over partitions, face nail | 3-10d (3" × 0.128") | — |
| Ceiling joist to parallel rafters, face nail | 3-10d (3" × 0.128") | — |
| Rafter to plate, toe nail | 2-16d (31/2" × 0.135") | — |

# Image Description

The image is a black-and-white construction framing diagram comparing **platform framing** and **balloon framing** in a building wall/floor/roof section. It shows a vertical cross-sectional view from foundation to roof, with labeled framing members, drilling/notching notes, span limits, and code section references.

The diagram is divided visually into three lower labels:

- **PLATFORM FRAMING** on the left
- **INTERMEDIATE BEARING WALL** in the center
- **BALLOON FRAMING** on the right

The upper portion shows the roof and second-story framing.

---

# Overall Layout

## Top / Roof Area

At the top left and top right are sloped roof assemblies meeting inward toward the center. Each side includes a horizontal member at the wall top and a sloped roof line above.

Visible labels:

- **TOP PLATE** (left side near the upper wall)
- **RAFTERS AND CEILING JOISTS OR APPROVED ROOF TRUSS** (center top, with arrows pointing to both roof framing assemblies)
- **TOP PLATE—SEE DRILLING AND NOTCHING PROVISIONS SECTION R602.6.1** (right upper wall)

Centered below the roof framing is the label:

- **SECOND STORY**

---

## Middle / Floor Framing Area

A horizontal floor framing assembly spans across the image at about mid-height.

Visible labels in this area:

- **FLOOR JOIST—SEE DRILLING AND NOTCHING PROVISIONS SECTION R502.8,**
- **JOIST IS PERMITTED TO BE CUT OR NOTCHED BETWEEN THESE LIMITS**
- Two dimension notes above the joist:
  - **1/3 SPAN**
  - **1/3 SPAN**

On the right side of the wall framing:

- **WALL STUD—SEE DRILLING AND NOTCHING PROVISIONS SECTION R602.6**
- **JOIST NAILED TO STUD**
- **1 IN. × 4 IN. RIBBON CUT INTO STUD—SEE SECTION R502.6**

On the left side around the floor framing:

- **BOTTOM PLATE**
- **TOP PLATE**
- **BAND JOIST OR BLOCKING**

Near the center below the floor framing:

- **FOR BLOCKING AND BRIDGING—SEE SECTION R502.7**
- **BEARING WALL**

---

## Bottom / Foundation Area

The lower portion shows two foundation types and floor support conditions.

### Left side foundation

Visible labels:

- **BAND JOIST OR BLOCKING**
- **SILL PLATE**
- **BOTTOM PLATE**
- **JOIST**
- **CRAWL SPACE OR BASEMENT FOUNDATION**

### Center bottom

Visible labels:

- **LAP JOIST 3 IN. MIN. OR SPLICE—SEE SECTION R502.6.1**
- **INTERMEDIATE BEARING WALL**
- **SUBFLOOR**

### Right side foundation

Visible labels:

- **SEE SECTION R602.8 FOR FIRE BLOCKING**
- **MONOLITHIC SLAB-ON-GRADE FOUNDATION**

---

# Detailed Structural Description

## Left Section: Platform Framing

The left side illustrates **platform framing**. The wall for the lower story rises from a foundation with:

- a **SILL PLATE** resting on the foundation,
- **BAND JOIST OR BLOCKING** at the floor edge,
- a framed floor platform above,
- then a wall above that platform with:
  - **BOTTOM PLATE**
  - studs
  - **TOP PLATE**

At the second-story level, another framed floor platform supports the upper wall. The upper wall again terminates at a **TOP PLATE** beneath the roof framing.

This side emphasizes stacked wall framing where each story is built on top of the floor platform below.

---

## Center Section: Intermediate Bearing Wall

The center shows an **INTERMEDIATE BEARING WALL** supporting joists from above and below.

Visible labels around this area:

- **FOR BLOCKING AND BRIDGING—SEE SECTION R502.7**
- **BEARING WALL**
- **LAP JOIST 3 IN. MIN. OR SPLICE—SEE SECTION R502.6.1**
- **SUBFLOOR**
- **INTERMEDIATE BEARING WALL**

The lower joists appear to meet or lap over the intermediate support. The upper floor joist line includes notes about drilling/notching and allowable cut/notch regions.

---

## Right Section: Balloon Framing

The right side illustrates **balloon framing**, where long continuous wall studs run from the lower level upward past the floor line toward the roof.

Visible labels:

- **TOP PLATE—SEE DRILLING AND NOTCHING PROVISIONS SECTION R602.6.1**
- **WALL STUD—SEE DRILLING AND NOTCHING PROVISIONS SECTION R602.6**
- **JOIST NAILED TO STUD**
- **1 IN. × 4 IN. RIBBON CUT INTO STUD—SEE SECTION R502.6**
- **SEE SECTION R602.8 FOR FIRE BLOCKING**
- **MONOLITHIC SLAB-ON-GRADE FOUNDATION**

The floor joist is shown attached into the wall framing rather than resting on a full platform. A ribbon let into the studs supports the joist ends.

chunks.embed:
# Image Description

A black-and-white technical construction diagram titled:

**WALL CONSTRUCTION**

The image shows a framed wall section with labeled structural components, including top and bottom plates, studs, header, floor framing, foundation cripple wall, and anchorage details. There are also small detail sketches at the bottom for diagonal bracing and corner/partition posts.

---

## Main Wall Framing Diagram

The central drawing is a vertical section/elevation of a wood-framed wall assembly extending from the top plate down to the foundation wall studs and sill plate.

### Top of Wall

Visible labels and notes at the top include:

- **SINGLE OR DOUBLE TOP PLATE**
- **CUT PLATE TIED WITH 16 GAGE STEEL STRAP. SEE SECTION R602.6.1.**
- **STAGGER JOINTS 24 IN. OR USE SPLICE PLATES. SEE SECTION R602.3.2**

A vertical pipe passes through the top plate area, with the note:

- **FIREBLOCK AROUND PIPE**

### Wall Stud Area

On the left side of the wall framing:

- **WALL STUDS—SEE SECTION R602.3**

In the center is a framed opening with labels:

- **HEADER—SEE TABLES R502.5(1) AND R502.5(2)**
- **JACK STUDS OR TRIMMERS**

The opening is framed with vertical studs on each side and a horizontal header above.

### Floor Framing / Platform

At the lower portion of the wall:

- **BOTTOM PLATE**
- **SUBFLOOR**
- **SILL PLATE**

To the right side of the floor framing:

- **SOLID BLOCKING**
- **FLOOR JOISTS**

### Foundation / Lower Wall

Below the floor framing is a short framed wall over the foundation area, labeled:

- **FOUNDATION CRIPPLE WALL—SEE SECTION R602.9**
- **FOUNDATION WALL STUDS**

Anchorage note pointing to bolts at the sill/foundation connection:

- **ANCHOR BOLTS EMBEDDED IN FOUNDATION 6 FT O.C. MAX.**

Dashed lines indicate the foundation below grade or hidden structural extent.

---

## Lower Left Detail: Diagonal Brace

A small perspective sketch shows studs with a diagonal brace let into them.

Label:

- **1 IN. BY 4 IN. DIAGONAL BRACE LET INTO STUDS**

Below that is a paragraph note:

**APPLY APPROVED SHEATHING OR BRACE EXTERIOR WALLS WITH 1 IN. BY 4 IN. BRACES LET INTO STUDS AND PLATES AND EXTENDING FROM BOTTOM PLATE TO TOP PLATE, OR OTHER APPROVED METAL STRAP DEVICES INSTALLED IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE MANUFACTURER'S SPECIFICATIONS. SEE SECTION R602.10.**

---

## Lower Right Detail: Corner and Partition Posts

A heading appears above three small framing detail sketches:

- **CORNER AND PARTITION POSTS**

Below the sketches is a note:

**NOTE: A THIRD STUD AND/OR PARTITION INTERSECTION BACKING STUDS SHALL BE PERMITTED TO BE OMITTED THROUGH THE USE OF WOOD BACKUP CLEATS, METAL DRYWALL CLIPS OR OTHER APPROVED DEVICES THAT WILL SERVE AS ADEQUATE BACKING FOR THE FACING MATERIALS.**

The three small sketches depict different corner and partition framing configurations.

---

## Conversion Note at Bottom

At the bottom left of the image:

- **For SI: 1 inch = 25.4 mm, 1 foot = 304.8 mm.**

---

# Visible Text Summary Table

| Location | Text |
|---|---|
| Title | **WALL CONSTRUCTION** |
| Top left | **SINGLE OR DOUBLE TOP PLATE** |
| Top center | **CUT PLATE TIED WITH 16 GAGE STEEL STRAP. SEE SECTION R602.6.1.** |
| Top right | **STAGGER JOINTS 24 IN. OR USE SPLICE PLATES. SEE SECTION R602.3.2** |
| Upper left near pipe | **FIREBLOCK AROUND PIPE** |
| Left wall area | **WALL STUDS—SEE SECTION R602.3** |
| Right opening area | **HEADER—SEE TABLES R502.5(1) AND R502.5(2)** |
| Center opening | **JACK STUDS OR TRIMMERS** |
| Lower left floor area | **BOTTOM PLATE** |
| Lower left floor area | **SUBFLOOR** |
| Lower left foundation area | **SILL PLATE** |
| Right floor area | **SOLID BLOCKING** |
| Right floor area | **FLOOR JOISTS** |
| Right lower wall | **FOUNDATION CRIPPLE WALL—SEE SECTION R602.9** |
| Right lower wall | **FOUNDATION WALL STUDS** |
| Lower center | **ANCHOR BOLTS EMBEDDED IN FOUNDATION 6 FT O.C. MAX.** |
| Lower left detail | **1 IN. BY 4 IN. DIAGONAL BRACE LET INTO STUDS** |
| Lower left paragraph | **APPLY APPROVED SHEATHING OR BRACE EXTERIOR WALLS WITH 1 IN. BY 4 IN. BRACES LET INTO STUDS AND PLATES AND EXTENDING FROM BOTTOM PLATE TO TOP PLATE, OR OTHER APPROVED METAL STRAP DEVICES INSTALLED IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE MANUFACTURER'S SPECIFICATIONS. SEE SECTION R602.10.** |
| Lower right heading | **CORNER AND PARTITION POSTS** |
| Lower right note | **NOTE: A THIRD STUD AND/OR PARTITION INTERSECTION BACKING STUDS SHALL BE PERMITTED TO BE OMITTED THROUGH THE USE OF WOOD BACKUP CLEATS, METAL DRYWALL CLIPS OR OTHER APPROVED DEVICES THAT WILL SERVE AS ADEQUATE BACKING FOR THE FACING MATERIALS.** |
| Bottom left | **For SI: 1 inch = 25.4 mm, 1 foot = 304.8 mm.** |

---

# Overall Interpretation

This is a code-style wall framing reference diagram showing standard wood wall construction details. It includes:

- top plate splicing requirements,
- fireblocking around a pipe penetration,
- wall studs,
- framed opening with header and jack studs,
- bottom plate and subfloor,
- floor joists with solid blocking,
- sill plate and anchor bolts,
- foundation cripple wall and foundation wall studs,
- diagonal bracing requirements,
- and corner/partition post framing details.

The drawing is instructional and appears to reference building code sections such as **R602.3**, **R602.6.1**, **R602.9**, **R602.10**, **R502.5(1)**, and **R502.5(2)**.

# Image Description

The image is a simple black-and-white technical line drawing showing **three structural elevation sketches**. Each sketch depicts a vertical support column, horizontal floor/roof members, a sloped roof line, ground line, and a labeled vertical dimension indicating **H = HEIGHT**. The three diagrams appear to compare different loading conditions based on the number of roof and floor loads.

## Overall Layout

- **Top left:** one-story condition with **1 ROOF LOAD**
- **Top right:** two-level condition with **1 ROOF LOAD / 1 FLOOR LOAD**
- **Bottom center:** three-level condition with **1 ROOF LOAD / 2 FLOOR LOADS**

---

## Top-Left Diagram

This sketch shows:

- A sloped roof line descending from left to right.
- A horizontal line beneath the roof, extending from the left toward the vertical support.
- One vertical support column near the right side of the sketch.
- One horizontal platform/member near the bottom, extending left from the column.
- A small rectangular footing/base at the bottom right of the column.
- A slightly uneven ground line beneath the structure.

### Dimension Label

A vertical double-arrow dimension is shown between the upper horizontal line and the lower horizontal member.

Visible text:

- **H = HEIGHT**

### Load Label

Centered below the ground line:

- **1 ROOF LOAD**

---

## Top-Right Diagram

This sketch shows a taller vertical arrangement than the top-left diagram.

Visible elements:

- A sloped roof line descending from left to right above the structure.
- A horizontal line just below the roof, extending from the left to the vertical support.
- A tall vertical support column on the right.
- Two horizontal members/platforms extending left from the column:
  - one intermediate floor/platform
  - one lower floor/platform near the base
- A small rectangular footing/base at the bottom right of the column.
- A slightly uneven ground line.

### Dimension Label

A vertical double-arrow dimension is drawn between the intermediate horizontal member and the lower horizontal member.

Visible text:

- **H = HEIGHT**

### Load Labels

Centered below the ground line:

- **1 ROOF LOAD**
- **1 FLOOR LOAD**

---

## Bottom-Center Diagram

This is the tallest of the three sketches.

Visible elements:

- A sloped roof line descending from left to right.
- A horizontal line beneath the roof extending toward the vertical support.
- A tall vertical support column on the right.
- Three horizontal members/platforms extending left from the column:
  - one upper intermediate platform
  - one middle platform
  - one lower platform near the base
- A small rectangular footing/base at the bottom right of the column.
- A slightly uneven ground line.

### Floor Label

Text placed near the middle platform:

- **1 FLOOR**

### Dimension Label

A vertical double-arrow dimension is drawn between the middle platform and the lower platform.

Visible text:

- **H = HEIGHT**

### Load Labels

Centered below the ground line:

- **1 ROOF LOAD**
- **2 FLOOR LOADS**

---

# Visible Text in the Image

All visible text, as shown:

- **H = HEIGHT**
- **1 ROOF LOAD**
- **1 ROOF LOAD**
- **1 FLOOR LOAD**
- **1 FLOOR**
- **H = HEIGHT**
- **1 ROOF LOAD**
- **2 FLOOR LOADS**

---

# Tabular Summary

| Diagram Position | Structural Levels Shown | Dimension Label | Other Labels | Load Text |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Top left | Roof + lower member | **H = HEIGHT** | None | **1 ROOF LOAD** |
| Top right | Roof + 1 floor + lower member | **H = HEIGHT** | None | **1 ROOF LOAD** / **1 FLOOR LOAD** |
| Bottom center | Roof + 2 floors + lower member | **H = HEIGHT** | **1 FLOOR** | **1 ROOF LOAD** / **2 FLOOR LOADS** |

---

# Interpretation of the Drawing

The image appears to illustrate structural height spacing and loading cases for a vertical support system:

1. **Single roof load**
2. **One roof load and one floor load**
3. **One roof load and two floor loads**

In each case, the vertical spacing marked **H = HEIGHT** indicates the distance between adjacent structural levels.

# Image Description

The image is a black-and-white technical line drawing of a **framed wall section** shown in a 3D/isometric view. It illustrates vertical **studs** between a bottom plate and double **top plates**, with notes about allowable **bored holes** and **notches** in the studs.

## Main Components Labeled

- **TOP PLATES**
  - A label points to the double horizontal members at the top of the wall.

- **STUD**
  - A label points to one of the vertical framing members.

## Visible Notes and Text

All visible text in the image reads:

- **TOP PLATES**
- **STUD**
- **BORED HOLE MAX. DIAMETER 40 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH**
- **5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE**
- **NOTCH MUST NOT EXCEED 25 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH**
- **BORED HOLES SHALL NOT BE LOCATED IN THE SAME CROSS SECTION OF CUT OR NOTCH IN STUD**
- **5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE**
- **IF HOLE IS BETWEEN 40 PERCENT AND 60 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH, THEN STUD MUST BE DOUBLED AND NO MORE THAN TWO SUCCESSIVE STUDS ARE DOUBLED AND SO BORED**

## What the Diagram Shows

The wall framing consists of:

- A **bottom plate** running horizontally along the base.
- Multiple vertical **studs** spaced along the wall.
- **Double top plates** running horizontally across the top.

The drawing includes examples of:

1. **A bored hole in a stud**
   - Shown as a circular hole through the stud.
   - The note states:
     - **BORED HOLE MAX. DIAMETER 40 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH**
     - **5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE**

2. **A notch cut into a stud**
   - Shown as a rectangular cut-out on the side of a stud.
   - The note states:
     - **NOTCH MUST NOT EXCEED 25 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH**

3. **Restriction on hole and notch placement**
   - A note states:
     - **BORED HOLES SHALL NOT BE LOCATED IN THE SAME CROSS SECTION OF CUT OR NOTCH IN STUD**

4. **Larger bored hole condition**
   - Another bored hole is shown in a stud with a note stating:
     - **IF HOLE IS BETWEEN 40 PERCENT AND 60 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH, THEN STUD MUST BE DOUBLED AND NO MORE THAN TWO SUCCESSIVE STUDS ARE DOUBLED AND SO BORED**
   - A nearby dimension note again states:
     - **5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE**

## Layout/Visual Structure

- The wall is drawn diagonally from lower left to upper right in perspective.
- The **top plates** are shown as two stacked horizontal members.
- Several vertical studs are visible.
- One stud near the left side contains a **small bored hole**.
- Another stud lower left contains a **notch**.
- A pair of studs near the center-right appears doubled, corresponding to the note about larger holes requiring doubled studs.
- Arrows and leader lines connect each note to the relevant stud feature.

## Table of Rules Shown in the Diagram

| Feature | Requirement / Limit |
|---|---|
| Bored hole | **BORED HOLE MAX. DIAMETER 40 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH** |
| Edge distance for hole | **5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE** |
| Notch | **NOTCH MUST NOT EXCEED 25 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH** |
| Hole and notch relationship | **BORED HOLES SHALL NOT BE LOCATED IN THE SAME CROSS SECTION OF CUT OR NOTCH IN STUD** |
| Larger hole condition | **IF HOLE IS BETWEEN 40 PERCENT AND 60 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH, THEN STUD MUST BE DOUBLED AND NO MORE THAN TWO SUCCESSIVE STUDS ARE DOUBLED AND SO BORED** |
| Edge distance for larger hole example | **5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE** |

## Overall Interpretation

This is a framing guideline diagram showing allowable limits for **drilling holes** and **cutting notches** in wood wall studs, including:

- maximum hole size,
- minimum edge clearance,
- maximum notch size,
- prohibition against aligning holes with notches in the same cross section,
- and reinforcement requirements when hole size exceeds 40 percent of stud depth.

# Image Description

The image is a clean black-and-white technical line drawing of a **framed wall section** shown in a 3D/isometric view. It illustrates vertical **studs** between a bottom plate and double **top plates**, with notes about allowable **bored holes** and **notches** in wall studs.

## Visible Title Text

At the upper-right edge, partially visible text reads:

- **WALL C**

(The remainder of the title appears cut off.)

## Main Diagram

The drawing shows:

- A wall frame with:
  - **double top plates**
  - a **bottom plate**
  - several vertical **studs**
- Some studs contain:
  - **bored circular holes**
  - a **notch/cut** in one stud

Leader arrows point to components and dimensions.

## Visible Labels and Notes

### Component labels
- **TOP PLATES**
- **STUD**

### Hole and edge clearance note
- **BORED HOLE MAX. DIAMETER 60 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH**
- **5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE**

This note appears on the left side near a bored hole in a stud.

### Additional edge clearance note
- **5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE**

This same note appears again on the right side near another bored hole in a stud.

### Notch limitation note
- **NOTCH MUST NOT EXCEED 40 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH**

### Hole/notch location restriction note
- **BORED HOLES SHALL NOT BE LOCATED IN THE SAME CROSS SECTION OF CUT OR NOTCH IN STUD**

## Diagram Details

- The **top plates** are drawn as two stacked horizontal members running along the top of the wall.
- The **studs** are vertical framing members spaced along the wall.
- One stud near the left side contains a **round bored hole**.
- Another stud lower on the left side contains a **notch** cut into its edge, with a nearby bored hole shown in relation to the warning note.
- A stud toward the right side also contains a **round bored hole**.
- Dimension arrows indicate the required **5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE** clearance from the bored hole to the edge of the stud.
- The bored holes are shown as circular openings through the stud faces.
- The notch is shown as a rectangular cut-out from the side of a stud.

## Table of Visible Text

| Text | Context |
|---|---|
| WALL C | Partial title at upper right |
| TOP PLATES | Label pointing to the double top plates |
| STUD | Label pointing to a vertical stud |
| BORED HOLE MAX. DIAMETER 60 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH | Note describing maximum hole size |
| 5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE | Edge clearance note near left bored hole |
| 5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE | Edge clearance note near right bored hole |
| NOTCH MUST NOT EXCEED 40 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH | Note describing maximum notch size |
| BORED HOLES SHALL NOT BE LOCATED IN THE SAME CROSS SECTION OF CUT OR NOTCH IN STUD | Note restricting hole placement relative to notch/cut |

## Overall Interpretation

This is a framing/code-reference style illustration explaining allowable modifications to **wall studs**, specifically:

- maximum bored hole diameter,
- minimum edge distance for holes,
- maximum notch depth,
- and the prohibition against placing bored holes in the same cross section as a cut or notch.

chunks.embed:
# Image Description

The image is a black-and-white technical line drawing showing a framed wall detail with a pipe passing vertically through the wall assembly. The drawing appears to illustrate a structural or code-related reinforcement method for notched top plates in an **exterior or bearing wall**.

## Visible Labels and Text

The following text is visible in the image:

- **EXTERIOR OR BEARING WALL**
- **NOTCH GREATER THAN 50 PERCENT OF THE PLATE WIDTH**
- **TOP PLATES**
- **PIPE**
- **16 GAGE (0.054 IN.) AND 1.5 IN. WIDE METAL TIE FASTENED ACROSS AND TO THE PLATE AT EACH SIDE OF THE NOTCH WITH 3-8D NAILS EACH SIDE**

## Detailed Visual Description

- A horizontal wall framing member is shown in perspective.
- The top horizontal member is labeled **TOP PLATES** and appears to consist of double top plates.
- Vertical wall studs are shown beneath the top plates.
- A vertical cylindrical **PIPE** passes upward through the wall and through a notch cut into the top plates.
- The wall is identified as an **EXTERIOR OR BEARING WALL**, with an arrow pointing to the top framing member.
- The notch in the top plates is labeled **NOTCH GREATER THAN 50 PERCENT OF THE PLATE WIDTH**.
- On both sides of the notch, a flat metal strap/tie is shown attached across the cut area.
- A note points to this strap and states:  
  **16 GAGE (0.054 IN.) AND 1.5 IN. WIDE METAL TIE FASTENED ACROSS AND TO THE PLATE AT EACH SIDE OF THE NOTCH WITH 3-8D NAILS EACH SIDE**
- The metal ties are drawn as rectangular plates with a row of circular nail holes or fastener marks.
- The pipe extends downward below the top plates and upward above them.
- The studs beneath the top plates are drawn as vertical framing members, with one stud visible on the left and another on the right of the pipe.

## Structured Summary Table

| Element | Description / Text |
|---|---|
| Wall type | **EXTERIOR OR BEARING WALL** |
| Framing member | **TOP PLATES** |
| Penetration | **PIPE** |
| Notch condition | **NOTCH GREATER THAN 50 PERCENT OF THE PLATE WIDTH** |
| Reinforcement | **16 GAGE (0.054 IN.) AND 1.5 IN. WIDE METAL TIE FASTENED ACROSS AND TO THE PLATE AT EACH SIDE OF THE NOTCH WITH 3-8D NAILS EACH SIDE** |

## Interpretation of the Drawing

This drawing depicts a construction detail for an **exterior or bearing wall** where a **pipe** passes through the **top plates**, requiring a **notch greater than 50 percent of the plate width**. Because of the large notch, reinforcement is provided using a **16 gage (0.054 in.)**, **1.5 in. wide metal tie**, fastened across the notch on each side with **3-8d nails each side**.

| Category C (Ss ≤ 0.6g and   Sds ≤ 0.50g) or less than 110   mph | One story   Top of two or three story | Methods 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 or 8 | Located in accordance with Section R602.10 and   at least every 25 feet on center but not less than   30% of braced wall line for Method 3 or 45% of   braced wall line for Methods 2, 4, 5, 6, 7 or 8. |
|  | First story of two story   Second story of three story | Methods 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 or 8 | Located in accordance with Section R602.10 and   at least every 25 feet on center but not less than   16% of braced wall line for Method 3 or 25% of   braced wall line for Methods 2, 4, 5, 6, 7 or 8. |
|  | First story of three story | Methods 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 or 8 | Located in accordance with Section R602.10 and   at least every 25 feet on center but not less than   45% of braced wall line for Method 3 or 60% of   braced wall line for Methods 2, 4, 5, 6, 7 or 8. |
| Categories D0 and D1   (Ss ≤ 1.25g and Sds ≤ 0.83g)   or less than 110 mph | One story   Top of two or three story | Methods 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 or 8 | Located in accordance with Section R602.10 and   at least every 25 feet on center but not less than   20% of braced wall line for Method 3 or 30% of   braced wall line for Methods 2, 4, 5, 6, 7 or 8. |
|  | First story of two story   Second story of three story | Methods 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 or 8 | Located in accordance with Section R602.10 and   at least every 25 feet on center but not less than   45% of braced wall line for Method 3 or 60% of   braced wall line for Methods 2, 4, 5, 6, 7 or 8. |
|  | First story of three story | Methods 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 or 8 | Located in accordance with Section R602.10 and   at least every 25 feet on center but not less than   60% of braced wall line for Method 3 or 85% of   braced wall line for Methods 2, 4, 5, 6, 7 or 8. |
| Category D2 or less than   110 mph | One story   Top of two story | Methods 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 or 8 | Located in accordance with Section R602.10 and   at least every 25 feet on center but not less than   25% of braced wall line for Method 3 or 40% of   braced wall line for Methods 2, 4, 5, 6, 7 or 8. |
|  | First story of two story | Methods 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 or 8 | Located in accordance with Section R602.10 and   at least every 25 feet on center but not less than   55% of braced wall line for Method 3 or 75% of   braced wall line for Methods 2, 4, 5, 6, 7 or 8. |
|  | Cripple walls | Method 3 | Located in accordance with Section R602.10   and at least every 25 feet on center but not less   than 75% of braced wall line. |

# Image Description

## Title
**WALL CONSTRUCTION**

The image is a technical line drawing sheet showing two wall corner construction details. The page contains labeled framing diagrams with callouts for nail spacing, material installation, and notes.

---

## Overall Layout

- **Top right corner:**  
  **WALL CONSTRUCTION**

- The sheet contains two labeled details:
  1. **(a) OUTSIDE CORNER DETAIL**
  2. **(b) INSIDE CORNER DETAIL**

- **Bottom left note:**  
  **For SI: 1 inch = 25.4 mm.**  
  **Gypsum board nails deleted for clarity.**

- Along the far right margin, there are three short vertical black marks aligned roughly with the upper, middle, and lower portions of the page.

---

## (a) OUTSIDE CORNER DETAIL

This upper diagram shows an outside corner framing condition. A vertical wall framing member intersects a horizontal wall framing assembly at a corner. The drawing includes studs, sheathing/panel layers, and gypsum wall board, with arrows pointing to specific components and fastening schedules.

### Visible text and callouts

- **16d NAIL AT 12 IN.O.C.**
- **8d NAIL AT 6 IN.O.C. (ALL PANEL EDGES)**
- **8d NAIL AT 12 IN.O.C. ON ALL FRAMING MEMBERS NOT AT PANEL EDGES**
- **ORIENTATION OF STUD MAY VARY. SEE FIGURE R602.3(2)**
- **GYPSUM WALL BOARD INSTALLED IN ACCORDANCE WITH CHAPTER 7**
- **WOOD STRUCTURAL PANEL INSTALLED IN ACCORDANCE WITH TABLE R602.3(1)**
- **(a) OUTSIDE CORNER DETAIL**

### Diagram content

- A **vertical framed wall section** rises upward from the corner.
- A **horizontal framed wall section** extends to the right from the same corner.
- The framing members are shown with rectangular stud cavities and cross-bracing/X-like line symbols inside some members.
- The horizontal wall section has:
  - an upper layer indicated by the callout for **GYPSUM WALL BOARD INSTALLED IN ACCORDANCE WITH CHAPTER 7**
  - a lower/outer layer indicated by the callout for **WOOD STRUCTURAL PANEL INSTALLED IN ACCORDANCE WITH TABLE R602.3(1)**
- At the corner intersection, multiple arrows indicate fastening requirements:
  - **8d NAIL AT 6 IN.O.C. (ALL PANEL EDGES)**
  - **8d NAIL AT 12 IN.O.C. ON ALL FRAMING MEMBERS NOT AT PANEL EDGES**
- On the left side of the corner, another callout indicates:
  - **16d NAIL AT 12 IN.O.C.**
- A note above the horizontal wall points to the stud area:
  - **ORIENTATION OF STUD MAY VARY. SEE FIGURE R602.3(2)**

---

## (b) INSIDE CORNER DETAIL

This lower diagram shows an inside corner framing condition. A vertical wall framing member meets a horizontal wall framing assembly at an interior corner. The drawing again includes framing, panel layers, and fastening notes.

### Visible text and callouts

- **ORIENTATION OF STUD MAY VARY. SEE FIGURE R602.3(2)**
- **GYPSUM WALL BOARD INSTALLED IN ACCORDANCE WITH CHAPTER 7 (INTERIOR)**
- **WOOD STRUCTURAL PANEL INSTALLED IN ACCORDANCE WITH TABLE R602.3(1)**
- **16d NAIL AT 12 IN.O.C.**
- **8d NAIL AT 6 IN.O.C. ALL FRAMING MEMBERS AT PANEL EDGES AND 12 IN. O.C.ON ALL FRAMING MEMBERS NOT AT PANEL EDGES**
- **(b) INSIDE CORNER DETAIL**

### Diagram content

- A **vertical framed wall section** is shown on the left side, extending downward from the corner.
- A **horizontal framed wall section** extends to the right from the top of the vertical section.
- The corner is configured as an **inside corner**, with framing members nested differently than in the outside corner detail.
- The horizontal wall section includes:
  - **GYPSUM WALL BOARD INSTALLED IN ACCORDANCE WITH CHAPTER 7 (INTERIOR)**
  - **WOOD STRUCTURAL PANEL INSTALLED IN ACCORDANCE WITH TABLE R602.3(1)**
- A note near the upper framing states:
  - **ORIENTATION OF STUD MAY VARY. SEE FIGURE R602.3(2)**
- A left-side callout indicates:
  - **16d NAIL AT 12 IN.O.C.**
- A lower-right callout indicates the fastening schedule:
  - **8d NAIL AT 6 IN.O.C. ALL FRAMING MEMBERS AT PANEL EDGES AND 12 IN. O.C.ON ALL FRAMING MEMBERS NOT AT PANEL EDGES**

---

# Image Description

The image is a technical construction diagram showing **portal frame construction** details for braced wall panels. It includes two configurations:

- **Single portal frame (one braced wall panel)**
- **Double portal frame (two braced wall panels)**

The drawing is black-and-white and uses dimension arrows, callouts, and labeled framing/sheathing details. It appears to be a building code or framing detail sheet.

---

# Top Labels and Extents

At the top of the image are two horizontal dimension lines indicating header extents:

- **EXTENT OF HEADER**  
  **DOUBLE PORTAL FRAME (TWO BRACED WALL PANELS)**

- **EXTENT OF HEADER**  
  **SINGLE PORTAL FRAME (ONE BRACED WALL PANEL)**

Below these labels, the header is shown spanning across the framed openings/wall segments.

---

# Left Portal Frame Detail

The left side shows a framed wall segment with sheathing and a header above.

## Dimensions and Header

- **MIN. 3" X 11.25" NET HEADER**
- Horizontal dimension under the header:
  - **6' TO 18'**

On the far left side, a vertical dimension indicates:

- **MAX. HEIGHT 10'**

---

# Left-Side Notes and Callouts

The following visible text appears with arrows pointing to framing components:

- **FASTEN TOP PLATE TO HEADER WITH TWO ROWS OF 16d SINKER NAILS AT 3" O.C. TYP.**

- **1000 LB STRAP OPPOSITE SHEATHING**

- **FASTEN SHEATHING TO HEADER WITH 8d COMMON OR GALVANIZED BOX NAILS IN 3" GRID PATTERN AS SHOWN AND 3" O.C. IN ALL FRAMING (STUDS, BLOCKING, AND SILLS) TYP.**

- **MIN. WIDTH = 16" FOR ONE STORY STRUCTURES**  
  **MIN. WIDTH = 24" FOR USE IN THE FIRST OF TWO STORY STRUCTURES**

- **MIN. 2x4 FRAMING**

- **3/8" MIN. THICKNESS WOOD STRUCTURAL PANEL SHEATHING**

- **MIN. 4200 LB TIE-DOWN DEVICE (EMBEDDED INTO CONCRETE AND NAILED INTO FRAMING)**

- **SEE SECTION R602.10.6.2**

---

# Center Detail

The center portion shows a narrow wall/post condition beneath the header.

Visible labels include:

- **1000 LB STRAP**

- **MIN. DOUBLE 2x4 POST**

Near the base:

- **MIN. 1000 LB TIE DOWN DEVICE**

This center detail appears to represent the post and tie-down condition associated with the portal frame opening.

---

# Right Portal Frame Detail

The right side shows another framed wall segment with sheathing and a panel splice note.

The large note block reads:

- **TYPICAL PORTAL FRAME CONSTRUCTION**

- **FOR A PANEL SPLICE (IF NEEDED), PANEL EDGES SHALL BE BLOCKED, AND OCCUR WITHIN 24" OF MID-HEIGHT. ONE ROW OF TYP. SHEATHING-TO-FRAMING NAILING IS REQUIRED**

- **IF 2X4 BLOCKING IS USED, THE 2X4'S MUST BE NAILED TOGETHER WITH 3 16d SINKERS**

Near the lower right side:

- **MIN. 1000 LB TIE DOWN DEVICE**

---

# Structural Elements Visible

The drawing visibly includes:

- A continuous header across framed wall segments
- Vertical wall studs/posts
- Sheathing panels indicated by dotted patterns
- Tie-down devices embedded into the foundation
- Concrete footing/foundation at the bottom
- Straps at header/post locations
- A blocked panel splice detail on the right-side wall segment
- Dimension arrows marking width, height, and header extent

---

# Image Description

This image is a black-and-white construction detail drawing showing a **concrete stepped footing** with framing above it. It includes labeled structural elements, dimensions, notes, and a footing section designation.

## Visible Drawing Elements

- A long horizontal top framing member labeled:
  - **2x SILL PLATE**
- Another label pointing to the upper framing:
  - **2- 2x PLATE**
- A splice in the top plate is labeled:
  - **SPLICE**
- A horizontal dimension near the splice reads:
  - **2 FT. MIN.**
- A note above the top plate reads:
  - **WHERE FOOTINGS SECTION "A" IS MORE THAN 8 FT., PROVIDE METAL TIE 16 GA. BY 1.5 BY 4 FT. MIN., EACH SIDE OF SPLICE W/ 8-16d COMMON NAILS.**
- The foundation is labeled:
  - **CONCRETE STEPPED FOOTING**
- On the right side, the framed short wall is labeled:
  - **2x CRIPPLE STUD WALL**
- At the bottom, a horizontal dimension/section label reads:
  - **FOOTING SECTION “A”**

## Structural Layout Shown

- The drawing shows a **stepped concrete footing** descending in multiple levels from left to right.
- Above the footing is a framed wall/foundation support system.
- The topmost horizontal member appears continuous except for a **splice**.
- The splice is associated with the **2 FT. MIN.** dimension.
- The note specifies reinforcement requirements when **Footing Section “A”** exceeds **8 FT.**
- Several anchor bolts or hold-down style fasteners are shown extending down into the concrete.
- A **2x cripple stud wall** is shown on the right, supporting the upper plate over stepped portions of the footing.
- The footing profile is drawn with stepped horizontal and vertical transitions, with hatched soil or bearing surfaces beneath.

## Visible Notes at Bottom

- **For SI: 1 inch = 25.4 mm, 1 foot = 304.8 mm.**
- **Note: Where footing Section “A” is less than 8 feet long in a 25 feet total length wall, provide bracing at cripple stud wall.**

## Text Present in the Image

### Labels and Callouts
- **2x SILL PLATE**
- **2 FT. MIN.**
- **SPLICE**
- **WHERE FOOTINGS SECTION "A" IS MORE THAN 8 FT., PROVIDE METAL TIE 16 GA. BY 1.5 BY 4 FT. MIN., EACH SIDE OF SPLICE W/ 8-16d COMMON NAILS.**
- **2- 2x PLATE**
- **CONCRETE STEPPED FOOTING**
- **2x CRIPPLE STUD WALL**
- **FOOTING SECTION “A”**

### Bottom Notes
- **For SI: 1 inch = 25.4 mm, 1 foot = 304.8 mm.**
- **Note: Where footing Section “A” is less than 8 feet long in a 25 feet total length wall, provide bracing at cripple stud wall.**

## Table of Visible Text

| Type | Text |
|---|---|
| Label | 2x SILL PLATE |
| Dimension | 2 FT. MIN. |
| Label | SPLICE |
| Note | WHERE FOOTINGS SECTION "A" IS MORE THAN 8 FT., PROVIDE METAL TIE 16 GA. BY 1.5 BY 4 FT. MIN., EACH SIDE OF SPLICE W/ 8-16d COMMON NAILS. |
| Label | 2- 2x PLATE |
| Label | CONCRETE STEPPED FOOTING |
| Label | 2x CRIPPLE STUD WALL |
| Section Label | FOOTING SECTION “A” |
| Conversion Note | For SI: 1 inch = 25.4 mm, 1 foot = 304.8 mm. |
| Note | Note: Where footing Section “A” is less than 8 feet long in a 25 feet total length wall, provide bracing at cripple stud wall. |

## Overall Interpretation

This is a structural construction detail for a **stepped concrete footing** supporting framed wall elements above. It emphasizes:
- sill plate and double top plate placement,
- splice location and minimum spacing,
- metal tie requirements when **Footing Section “A”** exceeds **8 FT.**,
- and bracing requirements when **Footing Section “A”** is less than **8 feet** within a **25 feet total length wall**.
